title: Adderley Property Purchase Loan Act
text:
IV – 246] ADDERLEY PROPERTY PURCHASE LOAN
ADDERLEY PROPERTY PURCHASE LOAN
Act No. 63 of 1963
ADDERLEY PROPERTY PURCHASE LOAN
63 of 1963 An Act to authorise the raising of a loan by the
S.I. 113/1965 issue of debentures to the Royal Bank of Canada to be
used for the purpose of purchasing the Adderley
Property and the construction of a building thereon.
[Commencement 20th December, 1963]
Short title. 1. This Act may be cited as the Adderley Property
Purchase Loan Act.
Interpretation. 2. In this Act —
“debentures” means the debentures issued under this
Act.
Authority to 3. It shall be lawful for the Governor to raise
issue debentures locally by the issue of debentures to the Royal Bank of
not exceeding
Canada under this Act a sum not exceeding in the whole
£200,000.
two hundred thousand pounds, to be applied for defraying
expenses in connection with the purchase of the Adderley
Property situate on the western side of East Street in the
City of Nassau in the Island of New Providence, and the
construction of a building thereon.
Principal moneys 4. The principal moneys and interest secured by the
and interest, how debentures are hereby charged upon and made payable out
secured.
of the general revenue and assets of The Bahamas.
Amount of 5. (1) The debentures shall be for such sums or
debentures. denominations as the Governor shall deem advisable and
shall bear interest at the rate of five and one-half per
centum per annum and shall be redeemable over a period
of ten years at the rate of ten thousand pounds per annum.
(2) The balance of the principal sum outstanding at
the end of the said ten year period shall be paid in a lump
sum to the said Bank.
Cancellation of 6. Upon the repayment of the principal moneys
debentures. secured by any debenture such debenture shall be delivered
up to the Treasurer to be by him cancelled and destroyed
S.I. 113 of 1965.
in such manner as the Minister for Finance may direct.
STATUTE LAW OF THE BAHAMAS [Original Service 2001]

ADDERLEY PROPERTY PURCHASE LOAN [IV – 247
7. The debentures shall be in such form as the Form and
Governor shall determine and shall be signed by the signature of
Treasurer or such other person as may be designated for debentures.
the purpose by the Governor. They shall be countersigned
by the Colonial Secretary or such other person as may be
designated for the purpose by the Governor.
8. (1) Every debenture shall, before being issued, be Register of
registered in a Register Book to be kept for that purpose at debentures.
the Treasury. All transfers and cancellations of debentures
shall be noted in the Register Book.
(2) The Governor may, if deemed advisable, by Order
authorise the making and keeping of a duplicate Register
Book to be kept at such place as may be designated in the
said Order.
9. Debentures shall be transferable by an instrument Debentures to be
in writing in such form as shall be prescribed by the transferred in
writing.
Governor.
10. The interest accruing due on debentures shall be Interest when
payable half-yearly out of the Treasury and shall have the and how payable.
same preference over all other payments to be made out of
the Treasury as has hitherto been granted to interest
payable on debentures issued in The Bahamas.
11. The Minister for Finance shall appropriate out of Provision for
the general revenue and assets of the Colony in each year interest and
redemption of
during which interest on the debentures shall be payable
debentures.
such sums as shall be necessary to provide for the interest
S.I. 113 of 1965.
on the debentures as it becomes due, and shall in each year
in which any debentures are redeemable appropriate from
the same source such sums as may be necessary to provide
for the redemption of the principal sums represented and
secured by the debentures, and such sums shall from time
to time be placed to the credit of the Treasurer in an
account to be opened in the Treasury to be utilized by him
for such purposes.
12. The debentures issued under this Act and the No income tax
interest on such debentures shall not be liable to any on debentures.
present or future income or other similar tax.

IV – 248] ADDERLEY PROPERTY PURCHASE LOAN
Payment of 13. All expenses of and incidental to the issue of the
expenses of debentures and of and to the repayment of the principal
debentures.
moneys secured by the debentures and all moneys required
and all expenses incurred in carrying out the provisions and
objects of this Act shall be paid out of the Treasury by
warrant in the usual manner.
STATUTE LAW OF THE BAHAMAS [Original Service 2001]
